label: Simple stye without orbital or vision red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of simple stye, hordeolum, or localized eyelid bump.

No contact lens pathway, vision change, severe eye pain, photophobia, trauma, foreign body, herpes or zoster concern, orbital or periorbital cellulitis concern, pregnancy, or immunocompromised-host pathway.
exclusion_criteria: Vision change, severe eye pain, photophobia, pain with eye movement, proptosis, orbital or periorbital cellulitis concern.
Contact lens use, eye trauma, foreign body, chemical exposure, corneal ulcer or keratitis concern, herpes or zoster eye concern, acute glaucoma mimic.
Pregnancy, immunocompromised host, systemic illness, or specialist-directed ophthalmology plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, or eye care if symptoms are not improving, and sooner if any red flag develops.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports eyelid bump and stye framing, red swollen tender eyelid bump features, warm compress home care, and escalation for vision problems, worsening swelling, fever, or concerning eye symptoms. Autonomous Phase 1551-1600 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed simple stye or hordeolum without contact lens use, vision change, severe eye pain, photophobia, trauma, foreign body, chemical exposure, herpes or zoster concern, orbital or periorbital cellulitis concern, pregnancy, immunocompromised-host pathway, systemic illness, or specialist-directed eye plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of simple stye, hordeolum, or localized eyelid bump
no contact lens use, vision change, severe eye pain, photophobia, trauma, foreign body, chemical exposure, corneal ulcer, herpes or zoster, orbital or periorbital cellulitis concern
no pregnancy, immunocompromised-host, systemic illness, or specialist-directed pathway
